---
title: "RLMMM01: Byrne correlated domain-factor measurement model, wave 3"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

RLMMM01 is the Byrne analogue of `lrp-rli-mm-001` (#338 Phase B),
**measurement-only** per the 2026-07-16 sign-off: correlated unit-variance
domain factors over the wave-3 nine-measure battery â€” **reading**
{`basread`, `basspel`, `woco`}, **language** {`bpvs`, `trog`}, **memory**
{`basdig`}, **ability** {`bassim`, `basmat`, `basnum`} â€” fitted on the
complete-case cross-section (n = 75). The deliverables are the
loadings/communalities table and the domain-factor correlation matrix: the
modern, measurement-error-aware analogue of the paper's correlation tables,
and the construct-structure summary that precedes any coupling model (Phase
C). Wave 3 is used because it is the only wave carrying the full ability triad
(`basmat` is wave-3+ only).

::: {.callout-important title="Measurement / triangulation only â€” nothing causal"}

Every factor correlation is a **descriptive association** in an observational
cohort (`causal_status="none"`). There is deliberately **no structural leg**:
the factors â†’ gain question belongs to Phase D (`lrp-rlm-adj-001` /
`lrp-rlm-hs-001`), so this model never regresses an outcome on the factors.

:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Single-indicator memory factor (stated assumption)"}

Memory has one indicator (`basdig`; the paper's visual-recall measures are not
in the prepared extract), so its loading and residual cannot be estimated:
they are **fixed by an assumed reliability of 0.8**
($\lambda = \sqrt{0.8}$, $\sigma = \sqrt{0.2}$). Correlations involving the
memory factor scale with that assumption â€” read them as conditional on it.
Loadings are pooled across the three reading groups: measurement invariance is
**assumed, not tested**, at this n.

:::

## Model

For child $i$, the standardised Haldane-logit indicator vector
$Z_i \in \mathbb{R}^9$ is Gaussian with the factor scores marginalised out
(the mm-001 funnel fix, measure-preserving):

$$
Z_i \sim \mathrm{MVN}\!\left(0,\ \Lambda\,R\,\Lambda^{\top} +
\operatorname{diag}(\sigma^2)\right),
$$

where $\Lambda$ is the sparse 9Ă—4 loading matrix (each indicator loads only on
its domain; positive $\mathrm{HalfNormal}(1)$ loadings, $\mathrm{HalfNormal}(1)$
residuals, memory fixed as above) and $R$ is the 4Ă—4 factor correlation matrix
under an LKJ(2) prior. The indicator-factor correlation is
$\lambda / \sqrt{\lambda^2 + \sigma^2}$ and the communality
$\lambda^2 / (\lambda^2 + \sigma^2)$, exactly as in the RLI family. No LOO is
computed, matching the RLI corr-factor convention.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_corr_factor.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
